There are exactly two mayors who have been caught on video smoking crack cocaine.
In 1990, the mayor of Washington, D.C., Marion Barry, was arrested in a hotel room during a
sting organized by the FBI. And in 2013, the Toronto Police Force announced they had obtained
a video of Mayor Rob Ford engaged in what appeared to be illicit activities.
Besides this one critical fact, there are very few similarities between the two men.

and the city would even put it on promotional material. Except the UN never said anything like

It starts right here with the original patriarch.
Douglas Bruce Ford was born smack dab in the middle of the Great Depression as the youngest of nine.
His father died when he was young, and with his family deep in poverty, he dropped out of high school at 16 and began working.
After a few years in sales, he and a neighbor founded a company, Deco Labels.
It's almost too on the nose that a political family would come from a signage business, but that's the truth.
A few years go by, and Doug buys his partner out.
By the 70s, Deco Labels was reliably profitable, and by now he and his wife Diane, also involved in the business, have had four children.
The eldest two, Kathy and Randy, have spent most of their lives out of the public eye.
Kathy has struggled with addiction since at least the 90s, and was forced to endure some horrific acts of violence caused by a string of unstable partners.
Randy, who is nicknamed Blackjack because he almost never takes off his trademark cowboy hat, had been arrested 17 times by age 33, for reasons varying from theft all the way up to assault.
If not for the younger two siblings, the Ford family would not be a household name.
There was Doug Jr., and five years after him, there was little Robbie.
Although there was an age gap, Rob and Doug were extremely close.
If Doug was the ambition, Rob was the heart.
They overlapped a bit in school growing up, and if we believe Doug, Rob was the best student out of the Ford siblings.
Rob would help out Doug on his paper route, and Doug would buy him hockey cards in return.
It was not a given that Rob was destined for politics.
His earliest passion appeared to be football.
His dad sent him to a Notre Dame training camp one summer, and he eventually got accepted for football at Carleton U, which, I gotta say, great choice of school.
His love of football will, in a bizarre series of events, nearly cost him his job as mayor.
But the football career never quite took off since he didn't get much playtime on the field and he was homesick.
So after a semester or two, he returned home to work at the family business.
By the 90s, all three of the Ford sons were at Deco, although it was clear that Doug Jr. was the one being groomed to take over eventually.
Deco would eventually have offices across North America, and by the 2010s, it would be making $100 million in sales annually.
They had a massive house with a three-car garage.
They owned a yacht and had a condo in Florida.
And the future finance minister came to barbecues in their backyard.

He lived below his means, something he tried to instill in his kids.
We'd work 16 hours a day and Pops would say,
